prose: Fukui reactivity indices for furan computed at DFT, tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz, no re-optimization (single-point Fukui). N±1 states: cation charge +1 mult 2, anion charge -1 mult 2. Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). Integrity: trustworthy=true, sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. No warnings reported. Headline value is the highest f+ (most electrophilic site): C5 (atom index 4, 0-based), f+ = 1.9242931. The alpha-carbons (C4, C5) dominate the electrophilic and nucleophilic Fukui response, consistent with furan's known 2,5-reactivity in electrophilic aromatic substitution. Note: Mulliken partitioning with large basis sets (def2-TZVP) can yield Fukui values outside [0,1]; this is a known artifact of the charge scheme rather than a convergence failure.
